summary: Part of CMP's platform team — contributing to AI feature development, design-system migrations, client bug triage, and feature requests alongside the broader platform.

bullets.d: Contributed to the platform's multi-year migration from a legacy design library to Axiom (`@optiaxiom/react`, public design system). Authored 15+ V1→V2 component conversions in the open-source repo and 10+ adapter components in the product. Wrote the Migration Guide and Icon Contribution Guide used by other product teams.
Worked on the V3 token/color rollout in the product, including a module-federation singleton fix that eliminated duplicate React-context errors across micro-frontends.

Worked on end-to-end integration of an internal AI SDK. Migrated the backing service from Express to Hono, set up the AWS CodeArtifact publishing pipeline, and built the permissions layer and test suite.
Built a reusable event-tracking React hook and instrumented structured AI-context events across 10+ product surfaces.

Implemented backend block-context extraction for LLM prompts, frontend block-form interaction, and AI resource rendering for the platform's AI document interface — following a block-based UI pattern inspired by [Adaptive Cards](https://adaptivecards.io).

Contributed to the platform's Spotlight global search with status pills, keyboard activation (Cmd+K), search-result separation, and segment-event telemetry. Built during, and won, the **Optimizely Global Hackathon 2025** as part of a team.
Implemented Dark Mode across 15+ surfaces (modals, dropdowns, tables, library, notifications, plan share, asset upload); refactored to CSS variables and prevented bleed-through into legacy UI.

Resolved duplicate comment/task creation on rapid clicks (server + client), fixed duplicate in-app notifications, restored thread-reply notifications to original commenters, and added 8 missing i18n locale variants.
Reviewed 300+ pull requests across product client/server and AI services; served as onboarding buddy for new team members; participated in sprint planning, reviews, and technical design discussions.

summary: Worked under Dr. Denys Poshyvanyk on intelligent software tools — building NLP infrastructure for SE-specific code understanding.

bullets.d: Built a programming language-specific tokenizer to improve NLP model predictions for software engineering tasks, contributing to the lab's research on intelligent software tools.

summary: Full-stack engineering on internal tooling and an automated trading integration.

bullets.d: Built custom graphs and advanced data filters to enhance reporting capabilities.

Built a two-service ASP.NET Core webhook relay that ingests TradeStation alerts, validates request integrity, and routes by trade type to a file-based queue consumed by an automated trading bot; deployed on Azure App Service.
